findings.title: Ingest client now depends on external metrics init ordering and can panic before returning
findings.why: The new duration observers are package-global interfaces that stay nil until some other package calls `ingestmetric.InitIngestMetrics()`. `client.Ingest` now defers `ingestmetric.IngestAPIDuration.Observe(...)` on every exit path, so any caller that constructs `ingestcli` without importing and initializing `pkg/metrics` will panic instead of returning an error or success result.
findings.risk_if_unchanged: Unit tests and any non-server caller of `ingestcli` can fail with a nil-interface panic purely because metrics were not initialized first. The ingest path panics in the caller goroutine, turning a normal request outcome into a crash/fatal test failure; the write path logs a recovered panic and silently skips the metric.
findings.evidence: `ingestmetric` declares `WriteAPIDuration` and `IngestAPIDuration` as nil globals until `InitIngestMetrics()` assigns them in `pkg/ingestor/ingestmetric/metric.go:30-49`. That initializer is only wired through `pkg/metrics.InitMetrics()` in `pkg/metrics/metrics.go:101`, but `pkg/ingestor/ingestcli` itself does not import `pkg/metrics`. This same package's tests call `client.Ingest(...)` and read `ingestmetric.WriteIngestAPIDuration` directly (`pkg/ingestor/ingestcli/client_test.go:115-116`, `185-187`) without any local initialization, so the new `Observe` on line 273 can dereference a nil observer immediately.
findings.change_request: Can we make the ingest metrics package safe to use without process-global init ordering, for example by initializing the histogram/observers inside `ingestmetric` itself or by defaulting these observers to a no-op until registration? Please add a case that covers calling `Ingest`/`WriteClient` without importing `pkg/metrics`, so this panic path stays closed.
